claim: Larry Silverstein signed the 99-year net lease for World Trade Center Towers 1, 2, 4, and 5 with the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey on July 24, 2001 — approximately seven weeks before the September 11 attacks. The bid had been accepted in partnership with Westfield America, Inc. on April 26, 2001.

sources.label: Snopes — “Did a WTC Leaseholder Buy Terrorism Insurance Just Before 9/11?” (fact-check)
Wikipedia — Larry Silverstein
sources.quote: he signed the lease on July 24, 2001

sources.label: Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Lease date is widely reported (Snopes accepts the date as accurate; the conspiratorial framing — that the late timing implies foreknowledge — is what Snopes rates 'mixture' on). Primary record is the Port Authority lease filing; URL pending. The chronological proximity to 9/11 is a documented fact; the inference of foreknowledge from it is not.
